symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged 2-week fever with chills and drenching night sweats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A subacute febrile illness is the core systemic feature of endocarditis, especially with a valve lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful Osler's nodes on the finger pads

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osler's nodes are a recognised immunologic phenomenon (minor Duke criterion) strongly associated with endocarditis.
A predisposing valve lesion is a Duke predisposition criterion raising endocarditis probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constitutional decline - profound fatigue, myalgia and ~3 kg weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: B-symptoms accompany the smouldering systemic infection of subacute endocarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematuria and no acute chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of haematuria does not exclude endocarditis; renal embolic/immune involvement can still be subclinical.

symptoms.symptom_name: New harsh early diastolic murmur of aortic regurgitation, splinter haemorrhages, and tender splenomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A new regurgitant murmur with splinter haemorrhages and splenomegaly is a classic endocarditis constellation on examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive blood cultures and a valvular vegetation on transoesophageal echocardiography

investigation_details.investigation_name: Multiple blood cultures (before antibiotics) and transoesophageal echocardiography
investigation_details.expected_result: Positive blood cultures for a typical organism and an echocardiographic valvular vegetation - the two major Duke criteria
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Endocarditis cannot be confirmed or the organism identified, and starting antibiotics first would sterilise the cultures.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive cultures plus an echocardiographic vegetation are the diagnostic major criteria for endocarditis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Infective endocarditis on a bicuspid aortic valve
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Prolonged fever with night sweats, Osler's nodes, constitutional decline and a predisposing valve lesion together give a high clinical suspicion of endocarditis, mandating urgent hospital admission for cultures (before antibiotics) and echocardiography.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Multiple sets of blood cultures (before any antibiotics)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A major Duke criterion; must be drawn before antibiotics to identify the organism and confirm the diagnosis.
Demonstrates the valvular vegetation and assesses valve destruction/regurgitation severity.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR) and renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the diagnosis and assess systemic effect and organ involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical oral antibiotics started at home before cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would sterilise blood cultures, obscure the diagnosis and undertreat a serious endovascular infection - contraindicated before hospital cultures.
